summary: Aircraft maintenance and operating material is organized by chapter, section and subject. The ATA-style structure helps pilots and maintenance personnel locate system information, revisions, effective pages, supplements and source authority quickly.

references.locator: ATA 100 chapter — documentation, numbering and revisions

components.name: Chapter / section / subject numbering
components.description: A three-element numbering format separates system, subsystem and unit-level information so related material can be found consistently.

components.name: List of effective pages
components.description: Used to confirm that a manual chapter is complete and current before relying on it for study or operation.

components.name: Supplements and amendments
components.description: Supplements and amendments are only applicable when the aircraft equipment or modification status requires them.
